attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who targets early moves for intermediate sprint bonuses and classification points rather than stage victories. Calculated exits from breaks once objectives are secured.
team_tactics: Gazprom deploys him strategically in breakaways despite small squad sizes to control secondary classifications. Willingly sacrifices personal stage ambitions to deliver teammates when holding classification jerseys.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling stages with intermediate sprints where breakaways can survive long enough to accumulate points. Thrives in scenarios where secondary classifications are available.
vulnerability: Not a pure stage winner—lacks finishing power to convert breakaway presence into victories. Prioritizes classification points over stage glory, limiting aggressive racing in finals.
key_patterns: Dominates intermediate sprint classifications through calculated breakaway participation (41 vs 14 points UAE Tour)
Uses tactical exits (fake punctures) from breakaways after securing bonuses across multiple stages
Consistently sacrifices stage winning opportunities to optimize GC/classification position or deliver teammates
Gazprom relies on him for bonus/classification control even with minimal team numbers present
form_trajectory: neutral: Executing assigned tactical roles effectively but not showing ambition beyond secondary classifications
